User Input: What are the two roles on this platform?
Response: You can register as either a Job Seeker who can search and apply for jobs or as an Employer who can post job listings and review applicants.

User Input: Difference between Job Seeker and Employer
Response: A Job Seeker can browse and apply for jobs while an Employer can post jobs manage listings and accept or reject applications.

User Input: How can I search for jobs?
Response: Go to the All Jobs page from the navigation sidebar. You will see all available job listings. Use the search bar at the top to filter jobs by title.

User Input: How to find jobs?
Response: Go to the All Jobs page from the navigation sidebar. Use the search bar to filter by job title and browse the available listings.

User Input: Where can I see all job listings?
Response: Click on ALL JOBS in the sidebar to see every available job listing. You can search by title and see the category country city and posting date for each job.

User Input: How to filter jobs?
Response: Use the search bar on the All Jobs page to filter jobs by their title in real time.

User Input: How do I view job details?
Response: Click on the Job Details button on any job card in the All Jobs page to see the full job information including title category location salary and description.

User Input: What information is shown on a job listing?
Response: Each job listing shows the title category country city posting date salary and full description. Employer accounts will not see the Apply button.

User Input: How do I apply for a job?
Response: Open the job listing you want and click the Apply Now button. Fill in your name email phone number address cover letter and upload your resume then click Send Application.

User Input: How to submit a job application?
Response: Open the job listing and click Apply Now. Fill in the application form with your details and resume then submit.

User Input: What do I need to apply for a job?
Response: You need to fill in your full name email phone number address and cover letter and upload your resume in PDF JPG or PNG format.

User Input: What file formats are accepted for resume?
Response: You can upload your resume as a PDF JPG or PNG file when applying for a job.

User Input: Can I apply for multiple jobs?
Response: Yes you can apply to as many jobs as you like by visiting each job listing individually and submitting a separate application for each one.

User Input: Can I delete my job application?
Response: You can delete your own pending application from the My Applications page. Once reviewed you cannot delete it.

User Input: How do I withdraw my application?
Response: You can delete your pending application from the My Applications page. If already reviewed contact the employer directly.

User Input: Where can I see my submitted applications?
Response: Go to MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar. You will see all your submitted applications with their current status.

User Input: How to track my application status?
Response: On the My Applications page each application shows a status timeline with stages Applied Under Review Awaiting Decision Interview Scheduled and Final Decision.

User Input: What are the stages of an application?
Response: The application goes through these stages: Applied then Under Review then Awaiting Decision. If accepted it moves to Interview Scheduled and then Final Decision.

User Input: What happens after I apply?
Response: Your application goes to the employer for review. The status shows Under Review on your My Applications page. The employer can then accept or reject it.

User Input: What does it mean if my application is accepted?
Response: The employer has accepted your application and scheduled an interview. You will receive a notification and can see the interview date time and meeting link in My Applications.

User Input: What does it mean if my application is rejected?
Response: The employer has reviewed your application and decided not to move forward. You will see a Rejected status on your application card.

User Input: How will I know if I got the interview?
Response: You will receive a real time notification and your application status will update to show the interview date time and meeting link.

User Input: Where do I find my interview details?
Response: Go to MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar. If accepted you will see the interview date time and a clickable Meet link on your application card.

User Input: How do I join my interview?
Response: Go to My Applications or the Interview Dashboard. Click the Join Meeting link next to your scheduled interview to open the video call.

User Input: What is the Interview Dashboard?
Response: The Interview Dashboard shows all accepted applications with scheduled interviews. Job seekers can see their interview schedule and final result. Employers can see all candidates and set the final verdict.

User Input: How to access the Interview Dashboard?
Response: Click on INTERVIEW DASHBOARD in the sidebar navigation menu. It is available to both Job Seekers and Employers.

User Input: What is the final verdict?
Response: After the interview the employer marks each candidate as either Selected for the job or Not Selected. You will see this result in the Interview Dashboard and My Applications.

User Input: How do I know if I got the job?
Response: After your interview the employer will update the final verdict. If selected you will see a Selected badge and receive a notification.

User Input: How do I post a job?
Response: You must be logged in as an Employer. Click POST NEW JOB in the sidebar fill in the title category country city location salary type salary amount and description then click Create Job.

User Input: What details are required to post a job?
Response: You need to provide the job title category country city location salary type either fixed or ranged salary amount and a full job description.

User Input: What job categories are available?
Response: The available categories are Graphics and Design Mobile App Development Frontend Web Development MERN Stack Development Account and Finance Artificial Intelligence Video Animation MEAN Stack Development MEVN Stack Development and Data Entry Operator.

User Input: What salary types can I choose?
Response: You can choose between a Fixed Salary where you enter one amount or a Ranged Salary where you enter a minimum and maximum salary value.

User Input: Can I edit a job I posted?
Response: Yes go to VIEW YOUR JOBS in the sidebar click Edit on the job card make your changes and click the checkmark button to save.

User Input: How to update a job listing?
Response: Go to VIEW YOUR JOBS click Edit on the job you want to update make your changes and click the checkmark to confirm.

User Input: How to delete a job posting?
Response: Go to VIEW YOUR JOBS in the sidebar and click the Delete button on the job card you want to remove.

User Input: Can I mark a job as expired?
Response: Yes when editing a job in VIEW YOUR JOBS you can change the Expired field to TRUE to mark it as no longer active.

User Input: How to see the jobs I have posted?
Response: Click on VIEW YOUR JOBS in the sidebar. You will see all your posted jobs with a summary of total active and expired listings.

User Input: Where can I see applications for my jobs?
Response: Click on MY APPLICATIONS in the sidebar. As an Employer you will see all applications for your jobs with applicant details and resume thumbnails.

User Input: How do I accept an application as an employer?
Response: On the My Applications page click Accept and Schedule Interview on the applicant card. Fill in the interview date time and meeting link then confirm.

User Input: How do I reject an application?
Response: Click the Reject Application button on the applicant card in My Applications. The applicant will be notified automatically.

User Input: How do I schedule an interview?
Response: When accepting an application a popup appears. Enter the interview date time and meeting link and click Accept and Send Interview to confirm.

User Input: How do I set the final hiring verdict?
Response: Go to the Interview Dashboard find the candidate and click Select to hire or Not Select to decline after the interview has taken place.

User Input: What are notifications?
Response: Notifications keep you updated in real time about key events like new applications being submitted jobs posted and application decisions.

User Input: Where can I see my notifications?
Response: Click NOTIFICATIONS in the sidebar. You will see all your notifications with a summary of total unread and read counts.

User Input: How do I mark a notification as read?
Response: Click the green checkmark on any notification card or click Mark All as Read at the top to clear all at once.

User Input: What does the notification badge mean?
Response: The badge on the NOTIFICATIONS menu item shows the number of unread notifications. It updates in real time as new events happen.

User Input: What types of notifications are there?
Response: There are four notification types: New Job Posted shown to job seekers Application Submitted shown to employer Application Accepted shown to job seeker and Application Rejected shown to job seeker.

User Input: How do real time notifications work?
Response: The app uses Socket.IO so you receive notifications instantly without needing to refresh. As long as you are logged in updates arrive automatically.

User Input: What is on the Home page?
Response: The Home page shows a welcome hero section a How It Works section with steps for using the platform a Popular Categories section and a Popular Companies section.

User Input: What is the welcome greeting screen?
Response: The first time you log in you see a three slide welcome screen introducing Job Dekho communities expert answers and job opportunities. Click Next through the slides and Get Started to close.

User Input: How do I navigate the app?
Response: Open the sidebar by clicking the hamburger menu icon in the top left. It contains links to Home All Jobs My Applications Post New Job View Your Jobs Interview Dashboard Notifications and Logout.

User Input: How do I contact support?
Response: You can email our support team at support@jobdekho.com or call 123-456-7890.

User Input: I need help
Response: Contact our support team at support@jobdekho.com or call 123-456-7890. They typically respond within 24 hours.

User Input: How long does support take to respond?
Response: Our support team typically responds within 24 hours.

User Input: Is posting a job free?
Response: Basic job posting is free. For premium features please check our pricing page.

User Input: Is there any cost to use Job Dekho?
Response: Basic features including job search and applying are free. For premium employer features please check our pricing page.

User Input: What is Job Dekho?
Response: Job Dekho is a job portal where Job Seekers can search and apply for jobs and Employers can post jobs review applications schedule interviews and make final hiring decisions. It includes real time notifications and a built-in interview management system.

User Input: How does Job Dekho work?
Response: Job seekers register browse jobs and apply. Employers post jobs review applications accept or reject candidates and schedule interviews. The entire hiring process from application to final verdict happens within the platform.

User Input: What if I encounter an error?
Response: Contact our support team at support@jobdekho.com with details of the error you are experiencing.

User Input: Error while submitting application
Response: Check your internet connection and make sure all fields are filled and the resume file is in PDF JPG or PNG format. If the issue persists contact support@jobdekho.com.

User Input: How do I report a fake job?
Response: Click the Report button on the suspicious job listing or contact our support team immediately at support@jobdekho.com.

User Input: Can employers see my resume?
Response: Yes your uploaded resume is visible to the employer on the My Applications page as a thumbnail. They can click to view the full resume.

User Input: Tips for writing a cover letter
Response: Keep your cover letter focused on why you are a good fit for the role. Mention relevant skills and experience and keep it professional and concise.

User Input: Tips for a strong resume?
Response: Keep your resume clear and organised with relevant skills and experience highlighted. Visit our blog section for detailed resume writing guides.
